blocks.speaker: Crowd
blocks.content: A horn… is it?

blocks.speaker: Crowd
blocks.content: That's impossible. Shirone's Photon Cannon evolved during the battle. And yet you're saying we can't win?
Shirone pushed himself up from the ground and spoke.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We can't win.
His friends doubted their own ears.
Perhaps it was the first time they had heard such words since meeting Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Crowd
blocks.content: What the hell? Don't talk like a weakling.

blocks.speaker: Crowd
blocks.content: It's not weak talk. That guy… he's seriously strong.
Shirone admitted it honestly.
Had they known he was the disciple of the Grand Sorcerer, it would have made more sense, but even without that knowledge, they could feel his will in every spell.
The will to not lose. The will to crush humanity. The will to prove he was the best.
He had maximized the power of the Photon Cannon, but Harvest, borrowing Canis's mental strength, absorbed even that.
From then on, it was a brawl. A battle of attrition, exchanging dark powers and Photon Cannons to whittle each other down.
In the end, Canis succeeded in countering and pushed Shirone out of the forest.

blocks.speaker: Crowd
blocks.content: 'Someone that strong… why would they want to hurt people?'
If he had chosen evil for any reason, that would be one thing. Everyone makes mistakes.
But he couldn't accept that evil was strong. Strength was the product of effort, not mistakes.

blocks.speaker: Crowd
blocks.content: Why are you doing this? What did the students ever do to you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So when you're outmatched in strength, you resort to words? Typical pattern of weak nobles.
Since it wasn't true, Shirone didn't flare up.

blocks.speaker: Crowd
blocks.content: Answer me. What you're trying to do is terrible enough to be recorded in history. At least tell me why.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: History? Who cares about that? The abandoned just struggle to survive each day. If I don't kill someone, I die.

blocks.speaker: Crowd
blocks.content: Don't try to justify it with sophistry. No matter how hard your life was, everyone in the world has their own unbearable pain. Everyone carries something.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You. Have you ever eaten shit off the ground?
Shirone fell silent.
A cold wind blew.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: 'Canis…'
Arin's eyes filled with sorrow.
Those who hadn't lived in Radum could never understand what Radum was like.
It was another world, detached from this one.
Canis and Arin had been abandoned there. They didn't remember since when or why it was just the two of them.
But from the moment they became aware, they were there, surviving even as other children died or were sold off.
Canis had been sharp since childhood. Though just a powerless child, he showed firsthand how strong a human could become when talent met malice.
In Radum, teeming with villains, Canis wanted only one thing.
To protect Arin for one more day.
In Radum, women didn't exist. To be precise, girls who had passed infancy.
In a place where food was extremely scarce, powerless girls were consumed as soon as they were born.
But Arin had survived in Radum until the age of ten.
The only woman in Radum.
The one who protected that eerie symbol was Canis.
He always barked like a mad dog. If an enemy appeared, targeting Arin, he would charge at them, ready to die.
With Arin by his side, he couldn't join any organization. So they survived by scavenging through trash bins every day.
The truly absurd part was that in Radum, even trash had owners. They fought desperately, their flesh tearing, just to claim the scraps others had discarded.
After such fierce battles, Canis might barely obtain a moldy half-piece of bread.
Arin hadn't eaten for three days. And as the price for her three-day fast, Canis hadn't eaten anything for twenty days.
'Hah! Hah! I did it! I got it! I got the bread!'
Canis sprinted mindlessly through the alley.
Only after shaking off his pursuers did he lean against a wall and exhale.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: His consciousness blurred, and all he felt was hunger. He couldn't even remember how long it had been since he last ate.
At that moment, a single piece of bread caught his eye.
Canis swallowed hard, his eyes gleaming with madness.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: He wanted to eat it. To put it in his mouth.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: 'Right, I need strength to keep Arin alive. It's okay to eat this much. Arin can hold on a few more days.'
Thinking that, it seemed like a brilliant idea.
Could anything be more logical? Eat this, regain some strength, and then find more food. That's all.
Canis opened his jaw and brought the bread to his mouth.
His parched mouth flooded with saliva, and his hands trembled violently.
Guh!

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis clenched his eyes shut and tucked the bread into his chest. It was all a lie. What kind of strength could this pathetic bread give him?
What kind of strength could this pathetic bread give him?
Canis looked around like a madman, thinking of stuffing stones into his stomach.
He crawled toward feces someone had left at the alley's entrance.
Before any thought could form, he reached out, shoved it into his mouth, and forced it down.
Ugh! Ugh!

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: His tongue, mouth, stomach—his whole body rejected it. But Canis swallowed it.
Better than stones.
Feces were better than stones.
After struggling with his stomach for a long time, Canis returned to their hideout with weak steps.
Though still nauseous, he brightened upon seeing Arin and held out the bread.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin, ta-da! I got this.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Oh, really? Wow, amazing.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Haha, I figured out the trick. I can get more from now on. Eat up.
Arin looked at the bread with sorrowful eyes.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Canis, let's eat together.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I'm fine. I picked up stuff while wandering. Earlier, there was a big centipede—got some nutrients from that. You can't eat that, so you need this.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: I can eat it too. How long will you treat me like a child?
Canis gripped Arin's shoulder with tender eyes.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin, I know. You're a brave kid. But you shouldn't. Understand? The only reason I can endure this hellhole is because I can feed you like a human being. If you become like me, you might go mad.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: It was a line she had heard dozens of times before. And every time, Arin would pretend to relent and take a bite.
But this time was different.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Arin stared at Canis's lips with trembling eyes. Something was smeared there, and a smell lingered.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Canis, what… did you eat?
Canis panicked like never before.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Huh? Ahaha! It's cake. Cream got on me while I was licking it. Sorry for not telling you. I was so hungry…!
*Slap!*
Canis's face snapped to the side sharply.
Though he had been hit countless times by back-alley thugs, this was the first time a slap had hurt this much.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: A-Arin….

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: You bastard… How could you do this to me! Am I livestock to you? Are you raising me? And then you tell me to eat this? What the hell am I to you!

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin, it's not like that! This is my punishment! It has nothing to do with you!

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: I don't need any of this! I don't need this!
Arin threw the bread away.
Watching it roll in the dust, Canis turned back with an angry expression.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin! What the hell! Do you know how hard I worked to get this…!

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Never do that again. If you do, I won't stay by your side anymore.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: I'm sorry, Arin. Don't leave. You're all the family I have. You're the only reason I live.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Right. Let's live, Canis. We must live.
Overwhelmed, Canis could only nod repeatedly.
Canis recounted his past as if narrating someone else's life.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: We lived in hell. But our master saved us from there. He gave us food, the power to protect Arin, and even Harvest, the pinnacle of dark magic.
People's gazes turned to Harvest.
Though usually a chatty magical creature, this time it remained silent.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I see.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What kind of life you lived. But that doesn't justify killing people. Just because your life was harder doesn't make evil right.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Don't misunderstand. I'm not defending myself—I'm teaching you. How shallow your so-called justice is, how hypocritical the world you live in is.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It wasn't your precious justice that saved me and Arin. I'm just acting on what I believe.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hurting people won't give you anything. If you don't try to understand others first, even your past will never find solace.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Kik! Solace? You're spouting nonsense till the end. Let me tell you the situation. Our master will erase this school entirely. That includes your friends.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No. You won't hurt anyone. Except yourself.
As Shirone summoned photons above his hand, Harvest spread its broad palm to shield Canis.
But that wasn't enough—it stretched its other arm to envelop Canis completely.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What's wrong, Harv? No need to be so scared.

blocks.speaker: Harvest
blocks.content: Dangerous. His aura is strange.
Canis snorted.
He had already gauged Shirone's strength in the forest. For a magic school student, he was decent, but still just a greenhouse flower.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Hmph. Anyway, to me….
Canis's expression froze.
The Photon Cannon floating above Shirone's palm was vibrating at a terrifying rate.
Its power was even greater than the one he had used in the forest. But given his severe injuries, it couldn't be that he had been hiding his strength.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: 'What's going on? With this kind of power….'
Canis and Harvest's analysis was mostly correct. But there was one thing they overlooked: Shirone was an Unlocker who had opened the Infinite Domain.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Last chance. Release the mind control.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ridiculous. Even if I die, I'll follow our master's will. You can't break my conviction.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Return everyone's memories. If not….
The Photon Cannon emitted light explosively, transforming into a blindingly cold sphere of white light.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll have to hurt you.
